tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58279,"title":"[DataBoxEdge] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after MPG migration to TypeSpec","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` **Service directory:** `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` **Triggered by:** PR #57614 by `@haiyuazhang` ## What Changed PR #57614 migrated `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec (MPG migration). The migration introduced: - **3 new resource types**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` - **New custom models**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger` - **Significant API surface changes** to `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`, `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource`, and other existing resource types - **Modified enum type**: `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` and `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` were reworked (43 and 116 deletions respectively) ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1` section is empty** — the section exists but has no entries under "Features Added", "Breaking Changes", "Bugs Fixed", or "Other Changes" despite substantial API additions and removals - **README "Key concepts" section is generic** — links only to external Azure SDK docs with no DataBoxEdge-specific content describing available resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, etc.) - **README "Examples" section has no code samples** — only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) URL; no DataBoxEdge-specific snippets for common operations - **New resource types are undocumented** — `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, and `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` are not mentioned in README or CHANGELOG - **No `Samples/` directory** in the tests folder — no test-backed snippet files exist at all 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit** Update the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to document the changes introduced by the TypeSpec migration: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource` exposing device capacity information. - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource` exposing device network settings. - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` exposing device update summary. - Added new model types: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`. - Migrated the library code generation from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec. ### Breaking Changes - `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API. - `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` — **edit** Replace the generic "Key concepts" and "Examples" sections with DataBoxEdge-specific content. Replace the existing "Key concepts" section body: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` library provides access to the following main resource types: - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`**: Represents a Data Box Edge physical device. Supports CRUD operations as well as actions such as scanning for updates, triggering updates, and retrieving security settings. - **`BandwidthScheduleResource`**: Manages bandwidth schedules on a device to control network usage during specified time windows. - **`DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`**: Manages compute roles (e.g., IoT, Kubernetes) running on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`**: Manages add-ons associated with a role (e.g., Arc for Kubernetes). - **`DataBoxEdgeShareResource`**: Manages data shares (SMB or NFS) on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource`**: Manages storage accounts configured on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`**: Provides information about device resource capacity utilization. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`**: Provides read access to the device's network configuration. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`**: Provides a summary of available updates for the device. For general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure Resource Manager documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` Replace the existing "Examples" section body: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Examples The following sections show code snippets for common scenarios. ### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription ``` ``` ### Get a specific Data Box Edge device ``` ``` ### List bandwidth schedules for a device ``` ``` For more samples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new test file with sample code that the snippet tooling will inject into the README. #### New file: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListDevices() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}, Location: {device.Data.Location}"); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetDevice() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices(); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync("myDataBoxEdgeDevice"); Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}"); Console.WriteLine($" Model: {device.Data.ModelDescription}"); Console.WriteLine($" Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListBandwidthSchedules() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices().GetAsync("myDataBoxEdgeDevice"); await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules()) { Console.WriteLine($"Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } } } ``` **README snippet placeholder locations:** - Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices` - Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice` - Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections. After the edits above, verify: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — needs updating (replace generic link with service-specific content, as described in Step 1) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — needs updating (add snippet placeholders and create sample test file, as described in Steps 1–2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — injects snippet code from test file into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — not required unless public types were added beyond what the migration already exported 5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24557992035/agentic_workflow) · ● 442.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58279","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T09:33:41Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T09:33:41Z"}

await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules()) { Console.WriteLine($"Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } } } ``` **README snippet placeholder locations:** - Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices` - Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice` - Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections. After the edits above, verify: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — needs updating (replace generic link with service-specific content, as described in Step 1) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — needs updating (add snippet placeholders and create sample test file, as described in Steps 1–2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — injects snippet code from test file into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — not required unless public types were added beyond what the migration already exported 5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24557992035/agentic_workflow) · ● 442.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58279","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T09:33:41Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T09:33:41Z"}

Potential missing files from test split","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Core

### Describe the bug

# Description
We have a [file here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/splittestdependencies/tests/inputs/projects.txt) which is supposed to list all the test projects that need to be split into jobs and run during a core ci run.

It appears this file is manually maintained and has deviated from reality if I understand its purpose correctly.

Currently there are 129 track 2 test csproj files that were in the repo but not in the split file.
There are also 6 test csproj files that were in the split file but not in the repo

We might consider auto-generating this file and failing in ci if there is a diff with the checked in version to ensure these stay in sync.

Below are the details of those files

## Test csproj files that were in the repo but not in the split file.

## Test csproj files that were in the split file but not in the repo
```
$(RepoRoot)sdk\core\Azure.Core.Experimental\tests\perf\Azure.Core.Experimental.Performance.csproj
$(RepoRoot)sdk\monitor\Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter\tests\Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Demo.Tracing\Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Demo.Tracing.csproj

```

### Expected behavior

The file and reality should match

### Actual behavior

There are differences on both sides

### Reproduction Steps

I wrote a quick script to validate

```C#
namespace splitCheck
{
    internal class Program
    {
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            string repoMarker = "$(RepoRoot)";
            string repoRoot = @"D:\Git\azure4\azure-sdk-for-net";
            HashSet<string> splitFiles = new HashSet<string>(File.ReadLines($@"{repoRoot}\eng\scripts\splittestdependencies\tests\inputs\projects.txt"));
            HashSet<string> testProjFiles = new HashSet<string>();
            var root = new DirectoryInfo($@"{repoRoot}\sdk");
            foreach (var serviceDir in root.GetDirectories())
            {
                foreach (var projectDir in serviceDir.GetDirectories())
                {
                    if (!projectDir.FullName.Contains(@"\Azure.", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                        continue;
                    foreach (var subDir in projectDir.GetDirectories("*", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                    {
                        if (subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\.vs", StringComparison.Ordinal) || subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\.vs\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        if (!subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\tests", StringComparison.Ordinal) && !subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\tests\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        var csprojFiles = subDir.GetFiles("*.csproj");
                        if (csprojFiles.Length == 0)
                            continue;
                        testProjFiles.Add(csprojFiles[0].FullName.Replace($@"{repoRoot}\", repoMarker, StringComparison.Ordinal));
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the repo but aren't in the split file");
            int missingInSplit = 0;
            foreach (var file in testProjFiles)
            {
                if (!splitFiles.Contains(file))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInSplit++;
                }
            }
            Console.WriteLine();
            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the split file but aren't in the repo");
            int missingInRepo = 0;
            foreach (var file in splitFiles)
            {
                if (!testProjFiles.Contains(file) && !File.Exists(file.Replace(repoMarker, $@"{repoRoot}\", StringComparison.Ordinal)))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInRepo++;
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInSplit} files were in the repo but not in the split file");
            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInRepo} files were in the split file but not in the repo");
        }
    }
}
```

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates for `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` following a TypeSpec MPG migration: specifically, populating the empty `1.2.0-beta.1` CHANGELOG section and improving the README's Key Concepts and Examples sections to reflect the newly added resource types.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge`
**Affected API:** README and CHANGELOG documentation; new resource types `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1` section is empty despite substantial API additions and breaking changes from the TypeSpec migration
- README "Key concepts" section contains only a generic external link with no DataBoxEdge-specific content
- README "Examples" section has no DataBoxEdge-specific code samples
- New resource types introduced by migration are undocumented in README and CHANGELOG
- No `Samples/` directory in the tests folder

**Root ask:** Add CHANGELOG entries documenting new features and breaking changes, update README with service-specific Key Concepts and code snippet examples, and create a sample test file under `tests/Samples/`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue filed automatically after PR #57614 migrated the library from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec. No runtime bug is involved.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57614 to understand the full scope of API surface changes (new resource types, modified enums, removed members).
2. Update `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` — populate the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section with Features Added and Breaking Changes entries.
3. Update `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` — replace the generic "Key concepts" body with a listing of available resource types and add snippet placeholders in the "Examples" section.
4. Create `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs` with `[Ignore]`-annotated test methods containing `#region Snippet:` blocks.
5. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` to inject snippets into the README, then `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to verify correctness.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — the package name `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` clearly identifies this as a management-plane SDK.
- **Service:** `DataBox Edge` — the issue explicitly names the `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` package and `sdk/databoxedge/` directory.
- **Confidence:** High — both labels are unambiguously implied by the package name and service directory; no alternative labels were plausible.
</details>

Entries like `%CodeGen %Mgmt` (line 1221), `%SQL - VM %Mgmt` (line 1164), `%SQL %Mgmt` (line 1146), `%Service Fabric %Mgmt` (line 1134), and others required a specific service label not present in the predicted labels ("DataBox Edge") — all skipped. The first match was `%Mgmt` at line 903. The `%DataBox Edge` entry at line 698 was not reached because the scan stopped at line 903.
